artifacts.summary: 3-phase remediation complete: removed the single empty untracked stray stub under ~/Mail/specs/, fixed lib.nix's manifestDirDefault to an absolute XDG-state path with verified wrapper rebuild and override precedence, and added anchored notmuch new.ignore guards verified via built config inspection.

completion_data.completion_summary: Removed the single empty untracked stray stub at ~/Mail/specs/072_email_workflow_infrastructure_prereqs/manifests/, changed the five email wrapper binaries' manifestDirDefault from a repo-relative specs/072_... path to an absolute XDG-state path ($HOME/.local/state/email-agent/manifests) with verified rebuild and override precedence, and added 15 anchored notmuch new.ignore regex entries to silence non-mail-file indexing spam under ~/Mail. No git-tracked ~/Mail content was touched.

memory_candidates.content: Nix wrapper scripts built via ''...'' indented strings: a plain double-quoted Nix string default value (e.g. manifestDirDefault = "$HOME/.local/state/foo";) keeps $HOME literal in the Nix value; when interpolated into the ''...'' preamble it is emitted as literal bash source, so bash expands $HOME at binary runtime, not at Nix eval time. Useful pattern for moving a compiled-in default off a repo-relative or archivable path onto a stable absolute XDG path without needing a Home Manager module (config.xdg.stateHome) when the file is a plain expression with no config arg.

To verify a Nix-built wrapper's compiled-in default/override precedence without live mail mutation, use the wrapper's own read-only safety class (e.g. email-census) with a throwaway --account/env override that reaches mkdir -p before any network/IMAP call, or pass an invalid --account to confirm mkdir ordering; always realpath-resolve through the home-manager-generation/home-path symlink chain to reach the true built binary rather than trusting a stale /nix/store hit from an unrelated earlier build.
